library.name: Basic Usage Contrast
library.description: Contrast-focused library for high-frequency words and structures that learners often confuse in context.

items.title: because / since / as
items.coreExplanation: because/since/as 后面都接完整从句（要有主语+谓语），用来说明原因。because 表示最直接、最明确的原因；since 和 as 更像“既然/由于”，语气较轻，常把原因放在句首，后面用逗号隔开，再说主要结果。
items.usageNote: because 最中性、最常用，口语和书面都自然，适合回答“为什么”。since/as 常用于解释背景、双方都知道的理由，语气更委婉，也更常见于写作或较平稳的说明。不要把它们后面直接接名词；那要用 because of。句首用 since/as 时，原因从句后通常加逗号。初学者最安全：先用 Because + 完整句子, + 结果句。

slotSchema.label: 原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子：主语+动词，如 she was sick。常错：只放名词，如 because the rain。小例：because it was late

slotSchema.label: 结果主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放主要信息或结果，如 we stayed home。常错：写成残句。小例：we canceled the trip

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用 because / since / as。常错：想强调真正原因却用 as/since，语气会太轻。小例：Because I was tired, I went to bed early

commonErrors: wrong: because of he was sick；错因：because of 后面不能接完整从句；改法：用 because he was sick，或 because of his illness。
wrong: Because of the weather was bad, we stayed home；错因：because of 后面接了主谓结构；改法：Because the weather was bad, we stayed home. / Because of the bad weather, we stayed home.
wrong: We stayed home because tired；错因：because 后面缺主语和谓语，不是完整从句；改法：We stayed home because we were tired.
wrong: As I was very hungry. I made noodles；错因：把从句单独断成一句；改法：As I was very hungry, I made noodles.
wrong: Because I was sick, so I stayed home；错因：英语里通常不用 because 和 so 同时连接同一组因果；改法：Because I was sick, I stayed home. 或 I was sick, so I stayed home.
wrong: Since you ask why, because I was busy；错因：结构混乱，since 从句后缺主句，because 也挂空；改法：Since you asked, I’ll explain. I was busy. 或 I was busy because you asked me to finish it today.

contrasts.title: because vs because of
contrasts.note: 结构不同：because + 从句；because of + 名词/名词短语。场景上，because 更适合完整解释，because of 更适合简短说明。对比：because it rained / because of the rain。

contrasts.title: because vs so
contrasts.note: 结构不同：because 引出原因从句，so 引出结果分句。语气上 because 像“因为”，so 像“所以”，不要在同一句核心结构里重复。对比：I left because I was tired. / I was tired, so I left.

contrasts.title: because vs that's why
contrasts.note: 结构不同：because 后接原因，that’s why 后接结果句。语气上 that’s why 更像总结说明，常见于口语解释。对比：I left early because I was tired. / I was tired. That’s why I left early.

contrasts.title: because vs since
contrasts.note: 结构不同：两者都接从句，但 since 更常放句首作背景。语气上 because 更直接回答真正原因；since 更像“既然/由于大家都知道”。对比：Because he was sick, he stayed home. / Since he was sick, he stayed home.

contrasts.title: because vs as
contrasts.note: 结构不同：两者都接从句；as 也常放句首。语气上 as 最轻，常用于平稳叙述或写作，不太适合强调强烈原因。对比：Because I needed help, I called her. / As I needed help, I called her.

examples.sentence: Because I was tired, I went to bed early.
examples.translation: 因为我累了，所以我早早睡了。
examples.note: 最安全基础句型：Because + 原因从句, + 结果主句。

examples.sentence: I went to bed early because I was tired.
examples.translation: 我因为累了，所以早早睡了。
examples.note: because 从句放句尾也很常见，口语尤其自然。

examples.sentence: Since it was raining, we stayed inside.
examples.translation: 由于在下雨，我们待在室内。
examples.note: since 语气比 because 轻，更像交代背景。

examples.sentence: As the roads were icy, the school closed early.
examples.translation: 由于道路结冰，学校提前放学了。
examples.note: as 常见于较平稳的书面说明。

examples.sentence: I chose the train because it was cheaper.
examples.translation: 我选择了火车，因为它更便宜。
examples.note: 这里如果换成 as/since，强调“真正决定原因”的感觉会变弱。

examples.sentence: Because my phone was dead, I couldn't call you.
examples.translation: 因为我的手机没电了，所以我不能给你打电话。
examples.note: 日常生活中非常自然的解释。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: {reasonClause} 放完整原因句，如 I was sick
{resultClause} 放结果句，如 I stayed home
templates.exampleSentence: Because I was sick, I stayed home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为我生病了，所以我待在家里。

templates.label: 句尾原因模板
slotHints: 先说主要信息，更像自然口语
because 后仍要接完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: I stayed home because I was sick.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我待在家里，因为我生病了。

templates.label: 背景说明模板
slotHints: 适合双方都知道或容易接受的理由
句首从句后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: Since it was late, we took a taxi.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于时间晚了，我们打了出租车。

templates.label: 写作用较轻模板
slotHints: 适合较平稳、书面的说明
不要用来强调特别强烈、核心的原因
templates.exampleSentence: As the weather was bad, the game was canceled.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于天气不好，比赛取消了。

items.title: because / because of
items.coreExplanation: because 用来接“完整原因句”，后面必须有主语+谓语，如 because it was raining。because of 用来接“名词性成分”，如名词、代词、动名词短语：because of the rain / because of being late。两者都常放在主句后说明原因，也可放句首，后面通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气上 because 最直接、最常用，口语和写作都自然；because of 也很常见，适合把原因压缩成较短名词短语。适合解释原因、改写句子、做语法选择题。不适合把 because 后面只接一个名词，也不适合把 because of 后面接完整句子。放句首时，原因部分后通常加逗号；放句中时一般不加。初学者最安全的做法：先判断后面是不是“完整句子”。

slotSchema.label: 原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 放在 because 后，必须是完整句子，通常有主语和动词。常错：只放名词，如 because rain。例：because it was late

slotSchema.label: 原因名词短语
slotSchema.hint: 放在 because of 后，可以是名词、代词或名词短语。常错：放完整句子，如 because of it was late。例：because of the traffic

slotSchema.label: 动名词原因
slotSchema.hint: because of 后也可接动名词短语，表示某行为是原因。常错：写成 because of be late。例：because of missing the bus

commonErrors: × because the rain → 错在 because 后不能只接名词。√ because it was raining / √ because of the rain
× because of he was sick → 错在 because of 后不能接完整句子。√ because he was sick / √ because of his illness
× I stayed home because of it was snowing. → 错在 because of 后面是从句。改为 I stayed home because it was snowing.
× Because of was tired, I slept early. → 错在 because of 后缺少名词性成分。改为 Because I was tired, I slept early. / Because of my tiredness, I slept early.
× Because of late, I missed the train. → 错在 late 不是合适的名词短语。改为 Because I was late, I missed the train. / Because of being late, I missed the train.
× I went out, because it was sunny. → 错在主句后接 because 从句时一般不在 because 前随意加逗号。改为 I went out because it was sunny.

contrasts.title: because vs since
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接从句，但 because 更直接强调“原因”，since 常带“既然、大家已知”的感觉。场景上 because 更适合明确回答 why；since 稍正式或带前提感。对比：Because it was raining, we stayed in. / Since it was raining, we stayed in.

contrasts.title: because vs as
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句；as 通常语气更轻，常把原因当背景信息。场景上 because 更清楚、更安全；as 在句首写作里较常见。对比：Because I was tired, I left early. / As I was tired, I left early.

contrasts.title: because of vs due to
contrasts.note: 结构上都常接名词短语，但 because of 更口语、更通用；due to 更书面，常见于正式写作。场景上初学者优先用 because of。对比：The game was canceled because of rain. / The cancellation was due to rain.

contrasts.title: because of vs thanks to
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接名词短语；但 because of 只表示原因，中性；thanks to 常表示“多亏、由于”并带积极结果，有时也可讽刺。对比：We were late because of traffic. / We arrived early thanks to your help.

contrasts.title: because vs so
contrasts.note: 结构上 because 引出原因，so 引出结果，不能直接互换。场景上回答“为什么”用 because；说明结果用 so。对比：I stayed home because I was sick. / I was sick, so I stayed home.

examples.sentence: I stayed home because it was raining.
examples.translation: 我待在家里，因为当时在下雨。
examples.note: 最基础用法：because 后面是完整句子。

examples.sentence: Because of the rain, I stayed home.
examples.translation: 因为这场雨，我待在家里。
examples.note: 句首位置；because of 后是名词短语，后面加逗号。

examples.sentence: Because I was tired, I went to bed early.
examples.translation: 因为我累了，所以我早早上床睡觉了。
examples.note: 句首用 because 从句，适合清楚交代原因。

examples.sentence: The meeting was canceled because of a power failure.
examples.translation: 会议因停电而取消。
examples.note: 较像书面说明；原因被压缩成名词短语。

examples.sentence: We left early because the traffic was getting worse.
examples.translation: 我们早走了，因为交通越来越糟。
examples.note: 这里不能换成 because of the traffic was getting worse。

examples.sentence: She missed class because of being sick.
examples.translation: 她因为生病没去上课。
examples.note: because of 后可接动名词短语，但初学者更常用 because she was sick。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 先说结果，再用 because 接完整原因句；确认 because 后有主语和动词。
templates.exampleSentence: I stayed home because I felt sick.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我待在家里，因为我觉得不舒服。

templates.label: 句首原因模板
slotHints: 想先交代原因时使用；because 从句后通常加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: Because it was late, we took a taxi.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为晚了，我们打了出租车。

templates.label: 名词原因模板
templates.template: Main clause + because of + noun phrase.
slotHints: 如果原因能说成名词短语，就用 because of；不要在后面放完整句子。
templates.exampleSentence: The flight was delayed because of fog.
templates.exampleTranslation: 航班因大雾而延误。

templates.label: 写作压缩模板
templates.template: Because of + noun phrase, main clause.
slotHints: 写作中可先放较短原因；句首短语后通常加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: Because of heavy traffic, we arrived late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为交通拥堵，我们迟到了。

items.title: therefore / so / that's why
items.coreExplanation: 这个组表示“前面是原因，后面是结果”。so最常用，通常放在两个分句之间；therefore更正式，常放在句中并常有逗号；that’s why后面直接接结果性说明，常形成“That’s why + 完整句子/名词短语”。先说原因，再接这些结果连接词最自然。
items.usageNote: so语气最中性、最适合口语和日常写作；therefore偏正式，常见于说明文、邮件、报告；that’s why很口语，也常用来总结前因后果。它们适合“已知原因→引出结果”的场景，不适合直接拿来引导原因。标点上，so前常有逗号；therefore前后常有逗号或分号；that’s why常在新句开头。初学者最安全：原因句, so + 结果句。

slotSchema.label: 原因分句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整句子，说明原因。常见错误是只放名词短语。例：It was raining

slotSchema.label: 结果连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用so / therefore / that’s why。常见错误是把that’s why后面直接接动词原形。例：so

slotSchema.label: 结果部分
slotSchema.hint: 通常放完整结果句。that’s why后面最安全也放完整句子。常见错误是把therefore后面接半句残缺结构。例：we stayed home

commonErrors: Because it was raining, so we stayed home.｜错因：because和so一般不一起用来连接同一组因果。｜改法：去掉一个：Because it was raining, we stayed home. / It was raining, so we stayed home.
It was raining, that's why stayed home.｜错因：that’s why后面缺主语，不是完整句。｜改法：It was raining. That’s why we stayed home.
He was sick, therefore he not come.｜错因：therefore后面的结果句不完整，时态和助动词缺失。｜改法：He was sick; therefore, he did not come.
Due to it was late, we left.｜错因：due to后面不能直接接完整从句。｜改法：Because it was late, we left. / Due to the late hour, we left.
I missed the bus. So. I was late.｜错因：so不能单独成句悬空使用。｜改法：I missed the bus, so I was late.
That's why because I was tired.｜错因：that’s why表示结果，不再接because引导原因。｜改法：I was tired. That’s why I went to bed early.

contrasts.title: vs because / since / as
contrasts.note: 结构上，because/since/as后接原因；so/therefore/that’s why后接结果。语气上，because最直接，since/as较书面；so最口语。对比：Because I was tired, I left early. / I was tired, so I left early.

contrasts.title: vs because of / due to / thanks to
contrasts.note: 结构上，这组后接名词短语，不接完整句；本卡三项后面接结果句。语气上，due to更正式，thanks to常带积极结果。对比：Because of the rain, we stayed home. / It rained, so we stayed home.

contrasts.title: so vs therefore
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都接结果句；therefore前后更常配逗号或分号。语气上，so中性口语，therefore更正式。对比：It was late, so we left. / It was late; therefore, we left.

contrasts.title: so vs that’s why
contrasts.note: 结构上，so常放两分句之间；that’s why常另起一句，后接完整解释结果。语气上，that’s why更像“这就是为什么”。对比：I forgot my key, so I went back. / I forgot my key. That’s why I went back.

contrasts.title: therefore vs thus
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可引结果，但thus更书面、更少见于日常口语。语气上，therefore较正式但仍常见，thus更像论文风。对比：The road was closed; therefore, we turned back. / Thus, the plan failed.

examples.sentence: It was raining, so we stayed home.
examples.translation: 下雨了，所以我们待在家里。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：原因句, so + 结果句。

examples.sentence: I forgot my wallet. That’s why I went back home.
examples.translation: 我忘带钱包了，那就是为什么我回家了。
examples.note: that’s why常放在新句开头，总结前一句原因。

examples.sentence: The data were incomplete; therefore, the conclusion was unreliable.
examples.translation: 数据不完整，因此结论不可靠。
examples.note: 正式写作中常用分号+therefore,+结果句。

examples.sentence: She was sick, so she didn’t come; she was sick. Therefore, she didn’t come.
examples.translation: 她病了，所以她没来；她病了，因此她没来。
examples.note: 内容相同，但后句更正式，适合比较语体差异。

examples.sentence: The train was delayed, so I got to work late.
examples.translation: 火车晚点了，所以我上班迟到了。
examples.note: 非常常见的生活场景。

examples.sentence: He didn’t study much. That’s why he failed the test.
examples.translation: 他没怎么学习，这就是为什么他考试没通过。
examples.note: that’s why后面接结果句，表达“这就是……的原因”。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 前面放完整原因句；后面放完整结果句；so前常有逗号
templates.exampleSentence: I was tired, so I went to bed early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我累了，所以我早早睡觉了。

templates.label: 换位置总结模板
templates.template: [原因分句]. That’s why [结果分句].
slotHints: 先说原因，再另起一句用That’s why；后面最好放完整句
templates.exampleSentence: I missed the bus. That’s why I was late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我错过了公交车，那就是为什么我迟到了。

templates.label: 写作用正式模板
slotHints: 适合邮件、报告、说明文；therefore前常用分号，后常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: The file was corrupted; therefore, we could not open it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 文件损坏了，因此我们无法打开它。

templates.label: 较短正式模板
slotHints: 想分成两句时可这样写；注意Therefore后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: The weather was bad. Therefore, the event was canceled.
templates.exampleTranslation: 天气不好，因此活动取消了。

items.title: due to / because of / thanks to
items.pattern: due to / because of / thanks to + noun
items.coreExplanation: due to、because of、thanks to 都用来表示“原因”，后面通常接名词、代词或名词短语，不直接接完整句子。because of 最中性最常用；due to 偏正式，常见于书面通知、说明；thanks to 带“幸亏/多亏”这样的正面或带感谢色彩。它们常放在句中，也可置于句首，句首后通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: because of 适合日常口语和一般写作，最安全；due to 语气更正式，常见于公告、邮件、报告，不太像轻松聊天；thanks to 通常用于好结果，或说话人把结果看成值得感谢，不适合明显负面结果。放句首时后面常用逗号，放句中时直接接原因短语。初学者先稳用 because of + 名词短语；如果后面是完整主谓句，要改成 because + 句子。

slotSchema.label: 原因名词短语
slotSchema.hint: 这里放名词、代词或短语，如 the rain, traffic, your help。常错：后面直接放完整句子 ×because of it was late；应改为 because it was late / because of the delay。例：because of the rain

slotSchema.label: 结果分句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整结果句，如 we stayed home。常错：只有名词没有谓语，句子不完整。例：We stayed home because of the rain.

slotSchema.label: 原因短语选择
slotSchema.hint: 在 because of / due to / thanks to 中选。常错：把 thanks to 用在明显坏结果上。例：Thanks to your help, we finished early.

commonErrors: × because of he was sick → 错在 because of 后面不能直接接完整句子；改为 because he was sick / because of his illness。
× due to of the rain → 错在 due to 本身已含 to，后面不加 of；改为 due to the rain。
× thanks to the heavy traffic, I was late for the exam → 语义别扭，thanks to 通常带正面或感谢色彩；改为 because of/due to the heavy traffic。
× Because of, we canceled the trip. → 错在 because of 后面缺少原因名词短语；改为 Because of the storm, we canceled the trip.。
× The game was canceled because of was raining. → 错在 because of 后面用了动词结构；改为 because it was raining / because of the rain。
× Due to I missed the bus, I was late. → 错在 due to 后面不能直接接从句；改为 Because I missed the bus, I was late. / Due to missing the bus, I was late.。

contrasts.title: because + clause vs because of + noun
contrasts.note: 结构区别：because 后接完整句子，because of 后接名词短语。语气上 because 更直接，because of 在名词化表达里很常见。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / I stayed home because of the rain.

contrasts.title: due to vs because of
contrasts.note: 结构上两者后面都常接名词短语，但 due to 更正式，更常见于通知、报告、说明；because of 更中性，口语最自然。对比：The event was canceled due to bad weather. / We canceled the picnic because of the rain.

contrasts.title: thanks to vs because of
contrasts.note: 结构上都接名词短语；语气和场景不同：thanks to 通常表示正面原因或感谢意味，because of 只表示原因，不带褒义。对比：Thanks to your advice, I passed. / Because of your advice, I changed my plan.

contrasts.title: because of/due to vs therefore/so/that's why
contrasts.note: 前者先给原因短语，后者用来引出结果。结构不同：because of + 名词；therefore/so/that's why + 结果句。对比：Because of the snow, school closed. / It snowed heavily, so school closed.

contrasts.title: sentence-initial vs mid-sentence position
contrasts.note: 这三个短语都可句首或句中。句首更像先交代背景，书面感稍强；句中更口语、更直接。句首后通常加逗号。对比：Because of the noise, I couldn't sleep. / I couldn't sleep because of the noise.

examples.sentence: We stayed home because of the rain.
examples.translation: 因为下雨，我们待在家里。
examples.note: 最安全、最常用的基础用法。

examples.sentence: Because of the noise, I couldn't sleep.
examples.translation: 因为噪音，我睡不着。
examples.note: 句首位置；后面通常加逗号。

examples.sentence: The meeting was postponed due to technical issues.
examples.translation: 会议因技术问题而延期。
examples.note: 正式、书面、公告感更强。

examples.sentence: Thanks to your help, we finished the project on time.
examples.translation: 多亏了你的帮助，我们按时完成了项目。
examples.note: 表示正面结果，带感谢色彩。

examples.sentence: I missed the train because of the traffic, not because I left late.
examples.translation: 我误了火车是因为堵车，不是因为我出门晚。
examples.note: 用于和 because + 从句做清楚对比。

examples.sentence: Due to rain, the school sports day will be held indoors.
examples.translation: 由于下雨，学校运动会将在室内举行。
examples.note: 公告或通知里很自然。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: [Result] 放完整结果句
[Cause] 放名词短语，如 the rain / traffic / my illness
templates.exampleSentence: We were late because of the traffic.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们因为堵车迟到了。

templates.label: 句首原因模板
slotHints: [Cause] 放名词短语，句首后加逗号
[Result] 放完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: Because of the storm, the flight was canceled.

templates.label: 正式写作模板

[Cause] 用较正式的名词短语，如 technical issues / bad weather

templates.label: 积极结果模板

[Result] 通常是好结果，不要配明显坏结果

items.title: although / though / even though
items.coreExplanation: although、though、even though都用来表示“虽然……但是……”，后面必须接完整从句（主语+谓语），不能直接接名词。它们常放在句首引出让步，也能放在句中连接前后两部分。even though语气最强，though最口语。
items.usageNote: 语体上，although较中性偏书面，though更口语，even though用于强调“尽管事实如此”。适合表达转折、承认事实后再说结果，常见于对话、说明、写作。不适合后面只放名词短语；那时应用despite/in spite of。句首后通常加逗号，句中一般不加。初学者最安全：Although/Though + 从句, 主句。不要再在主句前加but。
sceneTags: 作文中承认事实后补充观点

slotSchema.label: 让步连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用although / though / even though。常见错误是随便互换语气：even though强调更强。例：Even though it was late, we kept working.

slotSchema.label: 让步从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整从句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是只放名词，如× although the rain。例：although it was raining

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 主句写真正想表达的结果或态度。常见错误是再加but：× Although..., but.... 例：we went out

slotSchema.label: 句尾though
slotSchema.hint: though还可单独放句尾，表示“不过/可是”。常见错误是although放句尾。例：I liked it. It was expensive, though.

commonErrors: × Although the rain, we went out. → 错在although后不能直接接名词。√ Although it was raining, we went out. / √ Despite the rain, we went out.
× Although he was tired, but he kept studying. → 错在although已经表示转折，主句前通常不再加but。√ Although he was tired, he kept studying.
× Even though for two hours, he waited. → 错在even though后缺少完整从句。√ Even though he waited for two hours, he stayed calm.
× I didn’t go, although. → 错在although一般不能像though那样单独放句尾。√ I didn’t go, though.
× Although it was cheap. I didn’t buy it. → 错在把从句单独断成句，结构不完整。√ Although it was cheap, I didn’t buy it.
× Though formal report, the results are useful. → 错在though后不是完整从句。√ Though the report is formal, the results are useful.

contrasts.title: although vs despite / in spite of
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although后接从句；despite/in spite of后接名词或动名词。语气都可中性。对比：Although he was sick, he came. / Despite his illness, he came.

contrasts.title: although vs but
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although引导从句，but连接两个并列部分。although常放句首或句中；but更直接口语。不要同句重复：√ Although it rained, we went out. / √ It rained, but we went out.

contrasts.title: although vs however
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although后直接接从句；however多作连接副词，常另起分句并配标点。however更书面。对比：Although he is young, he is calm. / He is young; however, he is calm.

contrasts.title: although vs while / whereas
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although表示“虽然”；while/whereas更常作对比两种情况。while可较中性，whereas更书面。对比：Although it was hard, I finished it. / Whereas Tom is quiet, Ben is talkative.

contrasts.title: though vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构相近，都接从句；但even though让步更强，更强调“尽管事实如此”。though更轻、更口语，还能放句尾。对比：Though I was busy, I called. / Even though I was busy, I called.

examples.sentence: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: 最安全基础句型：句首让步从句 + 逗号 + 主句。

examples.sentence: I went for a walk although it was raining.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我还是去散步了。
examples.note: 同一意思也可把although从句放句末，句中一般不加逗号。

examples.sentence: Although the data are limited, the report offers a useful overview.
examples.translation: 虽然数据有限，这份报告还是提供了有用的概览。
examples.note: although常见于说明文和较正式写作。

examples.sentence: Even though he apologized, she was still upset.
examples.translation: 尽管他道歉了，她还是很难过。
examples.note: even though比although/though更强调“即使事实已经如此”。

examples.sentence: Though I was tired, I finished my homework.
examples.translation: 虽然我很累，我还是完成了作业。
examples.note: though更口语，语气比even though轻。

examples.sentence: The movie was too long. I liked it, though.
examples.translation: 这部电影太长了，不过我还是喜欢。
examples.note: 只有though能自然地放在句尾，表示补充转折。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 前面放完整让步从句
后面放真实结果，不要再加but
templates.exampleSentence: Although she was nervous, she gave the presentation well.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然她很紧张，她还是把展示做得很好。

templates.label: 句末从句模板
slotHints: 先说主要信息
句中连接时通常不加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: We kept talking though it was very late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然已经很晚了，我们还是继续聊天。

templates.label: 强让步强调模板
templates.template: Even though + 从句, + 主句
slotHints: 用于强调“尽管事实明确如此”
适合表达意外结果
templates.exampleSentence: Even though the test was difficult, he stayed calm.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管考试很难，他还是保持冷静。

templates.label: 句尾though模板
templates.template: 陈述句. + 另一句, though.
slotHints: 只适用于though
表示“不过/可是”，很口语
templates.exampleSentence: It was expensive. I bought it, though.
templates.exampleTranslation: 它很贵，不过我还是买了。

items.title: although / despite / in spite of
items.pattern: although + clause / despite + noun / in spite of + noun
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来表达“虽然/尽管……，但是……”。although后面必须接完整从句，至少有主语和谓语；despite和in spite of后面接名词、代词或动名词，不能直接接完整句子。它们常放在句首引出让步，也可放在主句后作补充。
items.usageNote: 语气上，although中性、很常用；despite和in spite of更像书面连接，写作里很自然。适合比较、解释结果与预期不一致、改写句子。口语里也能用，但随便聊天时though更轻松。句首后通常加逗号，句中一般不加。初学者先记住：although+完整句；despite/in spite of+名词或V-ing，别把两种结构混在一起。

slotSchema.label: 让步从句
slotSchema.hint: 放在although后，必须是完整句子：有主语+谓语。常错：although the rain。应说although it was raining。例：although she was tired

slotSchema.label: 让步短语
slotSchema.hint: 放在despite/in spite of后，可用名词、代词或动名词。常错：despite he was tired。应说despite his tiredness / despite being tired。例：despite the rain

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 写主要结果或事实，通常与前面形成“出乎预期”的对比。常错：只写前半句没有结果。例：we went out anyway

commonErrors: despite he was tired：错在despite后不能直接接完整句子；改为although he was tired，或despite being tired。
although the rain was heavy? 不对，若写although后面必须是完整句子，但the rain was heavy本身是完整句子，其真正常错是although the heavy rain；改为although it rained heavily，或despite the heavy rain。
in spite of she arrived late：错在in spite of后不能直接接从句；改为although she arrived late，或in spite of arriving late。
Although..., but...：英语里一般不把although和but并用，因为两者都表示转折；改为Although it was cold, we went out. 或It was cold, but we went out.
despite of the cost：错在despite后面不加of；只有in spite of有of。改为despite the cost / in spite of the cost。
although being tired：通常不自然，因为although最稳妥的是接完整从句；初学者改成although she was tired，或despite being tired。

contrasts.title: although vs though vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上三者都接完整从句；语气上though更口语，even though让步更强更强调事实。比较：Although it was late, we stayed. / Even though it was late, we stayed. 后者“尽管确实很晚了”更强。

contrasts.title: despite vs in spite of
contrasts.note: 结构上都接名词、代词或V-ing；意思几乎一样。语气上despite更简洁，常见于写作；in spite of稍长。比较：despite the rain = in spite of the rain。初学者按固定搭配记。

contrasts.title: even if vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句；但even if表示假设条件，even though表示真实让步。场景不同：计划/假设用even if，说明事实用even though。比较：Even if it rains, we’ll go. / Even though it rained, we went.

contrasts.title: although/despite vs but/however
contrasts.note: although/despite把“让步信息”放进从属结构里；but/however是并列或句间转折。写作时however更正式，前后常有标点。比较：Although he was tired, he worked. / He was tired, but he worked.

contrasts.title: although/despite vs while/whereas
contrasts.note: although/despite强调“尽管如此仍然”；while/whereas更常用于对照两种情况。语气上whereas更书面。比较：Although she is young, she is experienced. / Whereas she is young, her manager is older. 后句是对比，不是让步。

examples.sentence: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: 最核心用法：although后接完整从句。

examples.sentence: We went for a walk although it was raining.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: although从句也可放句末，句中通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: Despite the heavy rain, the game continued.
examples.translation: 尽管雨很大，比赛还是继续了。
examples.note: despite后接名词短语。

examples.sentence: In spite of being tired, she finished the report.
examples.translation: 尽管很累，她还是完成了报告。
examples.note: in spite of后可接动名词。

examples.sentence: Although he was inexperienced, he did the job well.
examples.translation: 虽然他经验不足，但工作做得很好。
examples.note: 和despite his lack of experience可互改，注意结构变化。

examples.sentence: Despite the traffic, I got to class on time.
examples.translation: 尽管路上堵车，我还是准时到了课上。
examples.note: 日常场景里也很自然。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
slotHints: {concessionClause}写完整句子，不要只写名词
{mainClause}写结果，常与预期相反
templates.exampleSentence: Although I was nervous, I answered the question.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我很紧张，我还是回答了那个问题。

templates.label: 短语型替换模板
slotHints: {concessionPhrase}可用名词、代词或V-ing，不可直接用完整句子
{mainClause}写主要结果
templates.exampleSentence: Despite the noise, I fell asleep.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管很吵，我还是睡着了。

templates.label: 句末位置模板
slotHints: 主句先说重点，后面补充让步信息
although后仍然必须是完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: She kept smiling although she was upset.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然她心里难过，她还是一直在微笑。

templates.label: 写作用模板
slotHints: 适合作文、报告、正式说明
句首短语后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: In spite of limited resources, the team achieved good results.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管资源有限，团队还是取得了不错的结果。

items.title: but / however / yet / still
items.coreExplanation: 这组词都用来表示“转折/对比”，把前后两个信息连起来，但位置和语气不同。but最常放在句中连接两个分句；however常放句首或分号后，后面通常有逗号；yet多放句中，表示“尽管如此仍然”；still可放句首或主语前，强调“还是、依然”。
items.usageNote: but最中性，口语和写作都常用；however更正式，适合邮件、报告、作文；yet带一点“出乎意料仍如此”的感觉；still更像说话人补充“即便这样也还是”。日常对话优先先学but和still，写作中再多用however。不要把however直接当and那样随便连两个完整句；放句首后常加逗号，放句中前面常有分号。初学者最安全：句子1, but 句子2。

slotSchema.label: 前一分句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整意思的分句。常见错误：只放单个词，导致but/however后面不平衡。例：I was tired

slotSchema.label: 转折连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用but/however/yet/still。常见错误：把however当并列连词直接无标点连接两个完整句。例：but / However, / yet / Still,

slotSchema.label: 后一分句
slotSchema.hint: 放与前文形成转折的完整分句。常见错误：内容不是转折而是并列，听起来不自然。例：I finished the work

slotSchema.label: 位置格式
slotSchema.hint: 安全格式：A, but B.；A; however, B.；A, yet B.；A. Still, B. 常见错误：句首however后忘逗号。例：However, I went anyway.

commonErrors: I was tired however I went out.｜错因：however通常不能像but一样直接无标点连接两个完整句。｜改法：I was tired, but I went out. / I was tired; however, I went out.
Although it was late, but I kept working.｜错因：although已经表示让步，后面通常不再加but。｜改法：Although it was late, I kept working. / It was late, but I kept working.
He is rich, still he is unhappy.｜错因：still连接完整句时更自然写成独立句或放主语前，逗号连接偏随意。｜改法：He is rich, but he is unhappy. / He is rich. Still, he is unhappy.
I studied hard, however I failed.｜错因：句首/插入式however后常要逗号，句中连接两个独立分句前常用分号。｜改法：I studied hard; however, I failed.
Yet I don’t know him well, but I trust him.｜错因：yet和but重复表达同一层转折，结构累赘。｜改法：Yet I trust him. / I don’t know him well, but I trust him.
We went shopping, however to buy food.｜错因：however后面需要完整分句，不接不完整目的短语。｜改法：We went shopping, but only to buy food. / We went shopping; however, we didn’t buy much food.

contrasts.title: but vs however
contrasts.note: 结构上，but直接连接两个分句最方便；however常放句首或分号后，更像“然而”。语气上，but更口语中性，however更正式。对比：I was busy, but I replied. / I was busy; however, I replied.

contrasts.title: but vs yet
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可连分句；语气上，yet更强调“出乎预期仍然如此”，比but更有反差感。场景上，but最通用，yet多见于书面或较讲究表达。对比：It’s simple, but useful. / It’s simple, yet effective.

contrasts.title: yet vs still
contrasts.note: 结构上，yet常作连接词连前后分句；still更常作副词放句首或主语前。语气上，yet偏书面转折，still偏说话人坚持原判断。对比：He knew the risk, yet he went. / He knew the risk. Still, he went.

contrasts.title: however vs although
contrasts.note: 结构上，however连接两个独立陈述；although引导从句，后面必须接完整从句。场景上，however适合句与句之间转折，although适合先让步再说主句。对比：It was raining; however, we left. / Although it was raining, we left.

contrasts.title: but vs while/whereas
contrasts.note: 结构上，but强调单纯转折；while/whereas更常用于平行比较两种情况。语气上，whereas更正式。对比：I like tea, but he likes coffee. / I like tea, whereas he likes coffee.

examples.sentence: I was tired, but I kept studying.
examples.translation: 我很累，但我还是继续学习。
examples.note: 最安全、最常用的基础转折句型。

examples.sentence: I was tired; however, I kept studying.
examples.translation: 我很累；然而，我还是继续学习。
examples.note: 同样意思，但更像书面表达。

examples.sentence: The plan is simple, yet effective.
examples.translation: 这个计划很简单，却很有效。
examples.note: yet突出“简单但出乎意料地有效”。

examples.sentence: It was raining. Still, we went for a walk.
examples.translation: 那时在下雨。尽管如此，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: still放句首，像补充“即便这样也还是”。

examples.sentence: He is very young, but he is already a good teacher.
examples.translation: 他很年轻，但已经是一位好老师了。
examples.note: 适合体会but的中性转折，与yet相比惊讶感更弱。

examples.sentence: The method has some limits; however, it is still useful in practice.
examples.translation: 这种方法有一些局限；不过，它在实践中仍然有用。
examples.note: 同时看到however和still：however连转折，still表示“仍然”。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: 前后都放完整分句
but前常有逗号
后一分句要有明显转折
templates.exampleSentence: I wanted to go, but I was too busy.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我想去，但我太忙了。

templates.label: 正式写作模板
slotHints: 前后都放完整句
however前常用分号，后常用逗号
适合作文、邮件、报告
templates.exampleSentence: The idea sounds risky; however, it may work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个想法听起来有风险；不过，它可能行得通。

templates.label: 意外反差模板
slotHints: 后一分句常是出乎预料的结果
比but更有“却”感
不要再和but一起用
templates.exampleSentence: The room is small, yet comfortable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个房间很小，却很舒服。

templates.label: 补充坚持观点模板
slotHints: 先说事实，再用Still补充“尽管如此”
Still后常加逗号
适合口语和叙述
templates.exampleSentence: I was nervous. Still, I gave the talk.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我当时很紧张。尽管如此，我还是做了演讲。

items.title: while / whereas
items.coreExplanation: while / whereas 用来把两个事实、情况或特点并列对比，意思接近“而、却、相比之下”。后面必须接完整从句（有主语+谓语）。它常放句首引出第一组对比，也可放句中连接前后两部分；whereas通常更书面。
items.usageNote: 语气以中性到较正式为主，适合比较数据、观点、两类人或两种情况，如作文、报告、分析说明。日常口语里 while 偶尔这样用，但 whereas 较少口语化。不适合只想简单转折一下的随意短句，那时常用 but。放句首时后面常加逗号；放句中时，前面通常先有一完整分句。初学者先安全使用“A..., while B...”即可。

slotSchema.label: 第一对比从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句：主语+谓语，如 Some students prefer studying alone。常见错误是只放名词短语。例：My brother likes tea

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 用 while 或 whereas。常见错误是把表示时间的 while 和对比用法混淆；如果意思是“当……时候”，就不是这张卡的用法。例：while

slotSchema.label: 第二对比从句
slotSchema.hint: 也要放完整从句，与前面形成清楚对照。常见错误是省掉谓语或只写形容词。例：my sister prefers coffee

slotSchema.label: 位置形式
slotSchema.hint: 常见两种：While/Whereas + 从句, 从句；或 从句 + while/whereas + 从句。常见错误是句首用了连接词却忘了逗号。例：While Tom works fast, Anna works carefully

commonErrors: wrong: whereas the older group more active → 错在 whereas 后面不是完整从句，缺少谓语。应改为: whereas the older group is more active
wrong: I like tea, whereas coffee. → 错在后半部分只有名词，没有完整陈述。应改为: I like tea, whereas my brother likes coffee.
wrong: While I was walking, my sister likes music. → 这里前半句是时间“当……时”，后半句却在做对比，逻辑混乱。要么都写时间关系，要么改成对比：While I like pop music, my sister likes jazz.
wrong: Whereas he is rich. He is unhappy. → 错在把从属连词 whereas 单独成句。应把两部分连在一起：Whereas he is rich, he is unhappy. 或 He is rich, whereas he is unhappy 不自然，最好改成 He is rich, but he is unhappy.
wrong: But some students study at night, whereas others in the morning. → 错在 others 后省略过头，初学者容易写成残缺结构。应改为: But some students study at night, whereas others study in the morning.
wrong: While, city life is convenient, country life is quieter. → 错在 while 后面多了不该有的逗号，打断从句。应改为: While city life is convenient, country life is quieter.

contrasts.title: vs but / however
contrasts.note: 结构上 while/whereas 后必须接完整从句；but 连接并列内容更灵活，however 常作副词单独插入。语气上 while/whereas 更像“并列对照”，较分析性；but 更日常直接。对比：Tom is outgoing, while Anna is quiet. / Tom is outgoing, but Anna is quiet.

contrasts.title: vs although / though
contrasts.note: 结构上二者都接从句，但 although/though 重点是“虽然……但是……”的让步；while/whereas 重点是“两相对照”。场景上 although 常表示出乎预期，while 更适合客观比较。对比：Although he is young, he is experienced. / While he is young, his manager is older.

contrasts.title: vs despite / in spite of
contrasts.note: 结构上 despite/in spite of 后接名词、代词或动名词，不接完整从句；while/whereas 后接完整从句。语义上 despite 表让步，while 表对比。对比：Despite his age, he runs fast. / While he is old, he is still active 不自然，若想让步应用 although/despite。

contrasts.title: vs time-meaning while
contrasts.note: 结构相同，都可接从句，但一个表示对比，一个表示时间“当……时”。场景上时间 while 常用于动作同时发生；对比 while 常用于比较两种事实。最小对比：While I was cooking, he called.（时间）/ While I enjoy cooking, he prefers eating out.（对比）

contrasts.title: while vs whereas
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句，都可句首或句中；语气上 whereas 更正式、更常见于论文报告，while 更常见也更自然。比较：While online classes are flexible, in-person classes offer more interaction. / Whereas online classes are flexible, in-person classes offer more interaction.

examples.sentence: My father likes hot weather, while my mother prefers cool weather.
examples.translation: 我爸爸喜欢炎热的天气，而我妈妈更喜欢凉爽的天气。
examples.note: 最基础的并列对比模板。

examples.sentence: While some students study best in the morning, others work better at night.
examples.translation: 有些学生早上学习效率最高，而另一些学生晚上效果更好。
examples.note: 句首用法；while 从句后加逗号。

examples.sentence: Whereas the first plan reduces costs, the second plan improves quality.
examples.translation: 第一种方案降低成本，而第二种方案提高质量。
examples.note: 较正式，适合报告或分析写作。

examples.sentence: Although this phone is cheaper, that one has a better camera, whereas this one has longer battery life.
examples.translation: 虽然这部手机更便宜，但那部手机摄像头更好，而这部手机的续航更长。
examples.note: 展示它和 although 可同句共存，但作用不同：although 表让步，whereas 表对比。

examples.sentence: City apartments are usually smaller, while houses in the suburbs offer more space.
examples.translation: 城市公寓通常更小，而郊区的房子空间更大。
examples.note: 适合客观比较两类事物。

examples.sentence: I like to plan everything in advance, while my roommate is more spontaneous.
examples.translation: 我喜欢提前把一切都计划好，而我的室友更随性。
examples.note: 日常表达中 while 比 whereas 更自然。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
templates.template: A + 谓语..., while B + 谓语...
slotHints: A、B 放主语；两边都写完整从句，不要只写名词。
templates.exampleSentence: My sister enjoys reading, while my brother prefers sports.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我姐姐喜欢阅读，而我哥哥更喜欢运动。

templates.label: 句首对比模板
templates.template: While/Whereas A + 谓语..., B + 谓语...
slotHints: 句首从句后加逗号；适合先提出第一项，再说第二项。
templates.exampleSentence: While some people save money carefully, others spend it freely.
templates.exampleTranslation: 有些人仔细存钱，而另一些人花钱很随意。

templates.label: 写作分析模板
slotHints: 适合图表、报告、议论文；whereas 语气更正式。
templates.exampleSentence: Whereas rural areas have fewer jobs, cities offer more opportunities.
templates.exampleTranslation: 农村地区工作较少，而城市提供更多机会。

templates.label: 数据对照模板
templates.template: A + 数字/特点..., while B + 数字/特点...
slotHints: 两边尽量比较同一维度，如价格、人数、速度。
templates.exampleSentence: The train takes two hours, while the bus takes three.
templates.exampleTranslation: 火车要两小时，而公交车要三小时。

items.title: if / unless
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来表示条件。if后接完整从句，意思是“如果……就……”；unless后也接完整从句，意思是“除非……，否则……”，本身已经带否定含义。条件从句常放在主句前，也可放在主句后；放前面时后面通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气通常中性，最常见于规则、提醒、日常安排、警告。unless很自然地用于“只有不……才会……”这类负条件；但如果条件本身已经是否定内容，初学者不要再用unless叠加not，容易变成双重否定。口语和写作都常见。把条件从句放句首时，后面一般加逗号；放句尾时通常不加。初学者最安全的做法：先写“If + 主语 + 动词, 主语 + will + 动词”或“Unless + 主语 + 动词, 主语 + will + 动词”。

slotSchema.label: 条件从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整句子：主语+动词，如 if it rains / unless you hurry。常见错误：只放名词或短语。可说 if you study hard，不要只说 if hard study。

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 写条件成立后的结果，常见 will/can/imperative，如 you will pass / call me。常见错误：结果不完整。例：If you leave now, you can catch the bus.

slotSchema.label: 否定内容处理
slotSchema.hint: unless本身已是否定条件，后面通常不用 not。常见错误：unless you don't go。应改为 unless you go，或 if you don't go。

commonErrors: wrong: Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late.｜错因：unless已经表示“除非不/如果不”，再加don't形成双重否定，意思混乱。｜改法：Unless you hurry, you'll be late. 或 If you don't hurry, you'll be late.
wrong: If not rain, we will go.｜错因：if后要接完整从句，不是只放not+词。｜改法：If it doesn't rain, we will go.
wrong: Unless it won't stop, we'll wait.｜错因：unless后再用won't不自然，逻辑也容易反。｜改法：Unless it stops, we'll wait. 或 If it doesn't stop, we'll wait.
wrong: You can't enter unless if you have a ticket.｜错因：unless和if不能连用在同一个条件标记位置。｜改法：You can't enter unless you have a ticket. 或 You can enter if you have a ticket.
wrong: Unless you will finish now, we start.｜错因：条件从句里初学阶段不要随便用will；主句时态也不完整。｜改法：Unless you finish now, we will start.
wrong: If you won't study, you fail.｜错因：这里是在说一般条件，不是“拒绝”；主句也最好用清楚的时态。｜改法：If you don't study, you will fail.

contrasts.title: if 与 unless
contrasts.note: 结构上二者都接完整从句，但unless自带否定意义。语气上if更普通、范围更广；unless更像“除非”，适合强调例外条件。对比：If you don't hurry, you'll miss it. = Unless you hurry, you'll miss it.

contrasts.title: if vs as long as / provided that
contrasts.note: 结构都可接从句，但as long as / provided that更强调“只要满足这个条件”，语气更明确，其中provided that更正式。if只是一般“如果”。对比：If you pay, you can enter. / As long as you pay, you can enter.

contrasts.title: unless vs except if
contrasts.note: 结构上都能谈例外，但unless更常见、更自然；except if较少见，常用于特别精确的说明。语气上unless更口语和普通。对比：I go out unless it rains. 比 I go out except if it rains. 更自然。

contrasts.title: if vs in case
contrasts.note: 结构相似，都可接从句，但场景不同：if表示“条件真的发生时”，in case表示“以防万一，为预防而做”。不要互换。对比：Take an umbrella if it rains.＝下雨时用；Take an umbrella in case it rains.＝怕下雨先带着。

contrasts.title: unless vs even if
contrasts.note: 结构都接从句，但unless表示“如果不……才……”，even if表示“即使……也……”，是让步，不是条件。语气上even if更强调结果不变。对比：Unless you call, I won't know. / Even if you call, I won't change my mind.

examples.sentence: If it rains, we will stay home.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就待在家里。
examples.note: 最基础的正条件：if + 从句，主句写结果。

examples.sentence: We will stay home if it rains.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就会待在家里。
examples.note: 条件从句放句尾也很常见，通常不用逗号。

examples.sentence: Unless you show your ID, you cannot enter the building.
examples.translation: 除非你出示身份证件，否则你不能进入这栋楼。
examples.note: 规则说明中很常见；unless强调“没有这个条件就不行”。

examples.sentence: If you don't leave now, you'll miss the train.
examples.translation: 如果你现在不走，你就会错过火车。
examples.note: 这是和unless可互换的典型句。

examples.sentence: Unless you leave now, you'll miss the train.
examples.translation: 除非你现在就走，否则你会错过火车。
examples.note: 和上一句意思接近，但unless更像在强调唯一避免坏结果的条件。

examples.sentence: Please keep this door closed unless staff need access.
examples.translation: 除非工作人员需要进入，否则请保持这扇门关闭。
examples.note: 较偏书面/告示风格，常见于通知、标牌。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: [conditionClause] 写完整条件，如 you study hard；[mainClause] 写结果，如 you will improve。
templates.exampleSentence: If you practice every day, you will improve.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你每天练习，你就会进步。

templates.label: 负条件模板
slotHints: [conditionClause] 不要再加 not，先写肯定形式，如 you hurry；[mainClause] 写不满足条件时的结果。
templates.exampleSentence: Unless you hurry, you will be late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 除非你快一点，否则你会迟到。

templates.label: 句尾条件模板
slotHints: 先写结果，再补条件；句尾的if从句前通常不加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: I will call you if I arrive early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果我早到，我会给你打电话。

templates.label: 书面规则模板
slotHints: 适合规则、通知、告示；主句可用祈使句或cannot/must等。
templates.exampleSentence: Do not open the window unless the teacher says so.
templates.exampleTranslation: 除非老师这样说，否则不要打开窗户。

items.title: if / as long as / provided that
items.pattern: if / as long as / provided that + clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来提出“条件”：if 表示一般“如果”；as long as 表示“只要……就”，强调条件足够即可；provided that 表示“倘若/前提是”，更正式。后面都要接完整从句，前面或后面都能放，放句首时后面通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气上，if 最中性、最常用；as long as 常见于口语协商、规则说明、做计划；provided that 多见于书面规则、协议、正式说明。它们都适合谈条件，不适合直接表达原因、时间或让步。放句首时常写“条件从句, 主句”；放句中一般不加逗号。初学者最安全的做法是先用“If + 主语 + 动词, 主语 + will/can + 动词”。

slotSchema.label: 条件从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整从句，要有主语和动词，如 if you finish early。常见错误是只放名词或短语，如 ×if your homework。小例：as long as it doesn't rain

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 这里放结果、安排、许可等主句，如 we can go out。常见错误是缺主语或只放一个动词，如 ×can go。小例：provided that you follow the rules, you may stay

slotSchema.label: 条件连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用 if / as long as / provided that。常见错误是把语气混用：想表达“只要”却只用 if。小例：If you need help, call me.

commonErrors: × as long as to finish early, we can leave.——错在 as long as 后面不能接 to do，必须接完整从句。应改为：As long as we finish early, we can leave.
× provided that you will pay, we ship today.——错在条件从句里滥用 will；这里说条件本身，通常用一般现在时。应改为：Provided that you pay, we ship today.
× If you are hungry. we can eat now.——错在标点；句首条件从句后应用逗号，不是句号。应改为：If you are hungry, we can eat now.
× We can go as long as if it stops raining.——错在重复连接词，不能同时用 as long as 和 if。应改为：We can go if it stops raining. / We can go as long as it stops raining.
× Provided that, you agree, we will start.——错在不必要地把 provided that 拆开。它应直接连完整从句。应改为：Provided that you agree, we will start.
× As long as = only if everywhere.——错在把两者完全当同义；as long as 常有“只要满足就行”的宽松感，only if 更强调唯一限制。可改成按语气选择：As long as you're careful, it's fine. / Only if you're over 18 can you enter.

contrasts.title: if vs as long as
contrasts.note: 结构上都接完整从句；但 if 只是一般“如果”，as long as 是“只要……就”，更强调条件足够。场景上 if 最通用，as long as 常用于协商、许可。对比：If you call, I'll help. / As long as you call, I'll help.

contrasts.title: if vs provided that
contrasts.note: 两者都可放句首或句中，后接完整从句；但 provided that 更正式，像“前提是”。适合协议、通知、规则，不太像随口聊天。对比：If you pay today, we can ship it. / Provided that payment is received today, we can ship it.

contrasts.title: provided that vs only if
contrasts.note: 结构上都可表达严格条件；only if 常直接强调“只有在……才”，限制更强，句型变化也更多；provided that 较书面，像补充前提。对比：You can go provided that you tell me. / You can go only if you tell me.

contrasts.title: if vs unless
contrasts.note: if 表示正条件“如果……”，unless 表示否定条件“除非……不”。结构都接从句，但语义方向不同。场景上 unless 常用于规则提醒。对比：If you hurry, you'll catch it. / Unless you hurry, you'll miss it.

contrasts.title: as long as vs when
contrasts.note: as long as 表条件，when 表时间。结构都能接从句，但意思不同。不要把“只要”误写成“当……时候”。对比：As long as you're ready, we'll start. / When you're ready, we'll start.

examples.sentence: If it rains, we'll stay home.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就待在家。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的条件句模板。

examples.sentence: We'll go for a walk if you finish your homework.
examples.translation: 如果你做完作业，我们就去散步。
examples.note: 条件从句放句末时通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: As long as you're quiet, you can stay here.
examples.translation: 只要你安静一点，你就可以待在这里。
examples.note: 表示满足这个条件就行，常用于许可或协商。

examples.sentence: Provided that all documents are complete, your application will be processed tomorrow.
examples.translation: 前提是所有文件都齐全，你的申请将于明天处理。
examples.note: 较正式，常见于书面通知或流程说明。

examples.sentence: If you need a ride, I can pick you up, but as long as you're ready by six.
examples.translation: 如果你需要搭车，我可以来接你，但前提是你六点前准备好。
examples.note: 同句对比 if 与 as long as：前者提一般条件，后者提明确要求。

examples.sentence: You may use the meeting room provided that you book it in advance.
examples.translation: 只要你提前预约，就可以使用会议室。
examples.note: 正式规则口吻；也可换成 if，但语气没这么正式。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
templates.template: If + 条件从句, + 主句
slotHints: 条件从句用一般现在时最安全，如 if you have time
主句可用 will/can/may + 动词，如 we can talk later
templates.exampleSentence: If you have time, we can talk later.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你有时间，我们之后可以聊。

templates.label: 句末条件模板
templates.template: 主句 + if + 条件从句
slotHints: 主句先说结果或安排，如 I'll help you
if 从句放后面时通常不加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: I'll help you if you're busy.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你很忙，我会帮你。

templates.label: 只要就行模板
templates.template: As long as + 条件从句, + 主句
slotHints: 适合表示许可、承诺、要求，如 as long as you are careful
主句常用 can / will / it's fine
templates.exampleSentence: As long as you're careful, it's fine.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要你小心一点，就没问题。

templates.label: 正式前提模板
templates.template: Provided that + 条件从句, + 主句
slotHints: 用于规则、协议、通知，语气较正式
条件从句后直接接主句，句首后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: Provided that payment is made today, we will ship the order tomorrow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 前提是今天付款，我们明天就会发货。

items.title: even if / even though
items.pattern: even if + clause / even though + clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构表示“让步”：前面的情况不会改变后面的结果。even if强调“即使……也……”的假设情况；even though强调“虽然……但是……”的已知事实。后面必须接完整从句，常放在主句前，也可放句中或句后。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性，口语和书面语都常见。适合表达决心、反驳、承认事实后继续说明结果。even if常用于还没发生、只是设想的情况；even though常用于说话人知道是真的情况。不适合只接名词或动词原形。若从句在句首，后面通常加逗号；初学者最安全的做法是先写“Even if/Even though + 主语+谓语, 主句”。

slotSchema.label: 让步从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整从句：主语+谓语，如 it rains / I am tired。常见错误是只放名词或短语，如 ×even if rain。例：even if it rains

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 这里放不受前面影响的结果或态度，如 I will go / we can finish it。常见错误是结果不完整。例：I will still go

slotSchema.label: 可选位置块
slotSchema.hint: 可用句首 Even if/Even though..., 主句；也可主句 + even if/even though...。常见错误是句首从句后忘记逗号。例：Even though he was late, he apologized.

commonErrors: ×Even if raining, I will go.——错在even if后面缺少完整从句。应改为Even if it rains, I will go.
×Even though it may rain tomorrow, we will cancel.——若只是猜测明天会不会下雨，更自然用even if。应改为Even if it rains tomorrow, we will cancel.／Even if it may rain tomorrow较少用。
×Even if he was tired, he still came yesterday.——这里说的是已知事实“他昨天确实累了”，更合适用even though。应改为Even though he was tired, he still came yesterday.
×I will go, even though it rains tomorrow.——若表示未来假设，不用even though。应改为I will go, even if it rains tomorrow.
×Even though of the high price, I bought it.——even though后不能直接接介词短语。应改为Even though it was expensive, I bought it. 或 Despite the high price, I bought it.
×Even if he is rich but he is unhappy.——英语里不用双重转折，even if从句后主句不要再加but。应改为Even if he is rich, he is unhappy.

contrasts.title: even if vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上两者后面都接完整从句；区别在于even if讲假设、让步，even though讲已知事实、让步。语气上，前者像“就算……也”，后者像“虽然……但是”。对比：Even if it rains, we’ll play. / Even though it rained, we played.

contrasts.title: even though vs although/though
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接从句。语气上even though更强，强调“尽管事实如此”；although较中性，though更口语。比较：Even though I was tired, I worked. 比 Although I was tired, I worked. 语气更强。

contrasts.title: even if vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句，但if只是普通条件；even if表示“这个条件成立也不影响结果”。语气上even if更强调结果不变。最小对比：If it rains, we’ll stay home. / Even if it rains, we’ll go out.

contrasts.title: even if vs unless
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句，但unless表示“除非……否则不……”，是真正限制条件；even if表示“即使……也……”，不限制主句结果。场景不同：unless常用于规则、条件；even if常用于决心。对比：Unless you hurry, you’ll miss it. / Even if you hurry, you may still miss it.

contrasts.title: even though vs despite
contrasts.note: 结构差别最重要：even though后接从句，despite后接名词/动名词。语气上两者都可表达让步，despite更紧凑、常见于书面。对比：Even though he was sick, he worked. / Despite being sick, he worked.

examples.sentence: Even if it rains, I will go.
examples.translation: 即使下雨，我也会去。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：假设情况+结果不变。

examples.sentence: I will go even if it rains.
examples.translation: 即使下雨，我也会去。
examples.note: 同样正确；让步从句放句尾更像补充条件。

examples.sentence: Even though he was tired, he finished the report.
examples.translation: 虽然他很累，但他还是完成了报告。
examples.note: 已知事实，所以用even though。

examples.sentence: Even if you apologize, she may not change her mind.
examples.translation: 即使你道歉，她也未必会改变主意。
examples.note: 和if相比，这里强调“道歉也不一定有用”。

examples.sentence: Even though it was expensive, we bought it.
examples.translation: 虽然它很贵，我们还是买了。
examples.note: 可替换为although，但even though语气更强。

examples.sentence: Even if nobody helps me, I can do it myself.
examples.translation: 即使没人帮我，我也能自己做。
examples.note: 常用于表达决心或独立。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: Even if/Even though + 从句, + 主句.
slotHints: 从句里放主语+谓语；假设用even if，事实用even though
主句写不会被前面情况改变的结果
templates.exampleSentence: Even if I am busy, I will call you.
templates.exampleTranslation: 即使我很忙，我也会给你打电话。

templates.label: 句尾让步模板
templates.template: 主句 + even if/even though + 从句.
slotHints: 先写主要信息，再补充让步条件
句尾也要接完整从句，不要只接名词
templates.exampleSentence: We can finish it today even though we are short on time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然时间不多，我们今天还是能完成它。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
templates.template: Even though + 从句, + 主句; however, + 补充句.
slotHints: 先承认事实，再写主要判断
however后通常另起一个分句，适合较正式写作
templates.exampleSentence: Even though the plan is risky, we should try it; however, we must prepare carefully.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然这个计划有风险，我们还是应该尝试；不过，我们必须认真准备。

items.title: in case / if
items.coreExplanation: in case表示“以防、免得”，主句通常是先做准备或采取措施，后面接完整从句说明可能发生的事；if表示“如果”，只是提出条件。两者都可放句首或句中，但in case最常见于主句后，说明做某事的目的。
items.usageNote: 语气上，if最中性，口语书面都常见；in case也常见，但带“预防、提前准备”的意思，适合出门、旅行、安全、提醒等场景。不适合把in case只当普通“如果”来用。句首时后面常加逗号，句中一般不加。初学者最安全的做法是先写“主句表示准备/带东西/留信息 + in case + 可能情况”。

slotSchema.label: 主句动作
slotSchema.hint: 放“提前准备、携带、保存、留着、写下、充电”等动作。常见错误是主句不是准备行为，结果不像in case。例：Take an umbrella...

slotSchema.label: 可能发生的情况从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句，表示可能发生但不确定的事。常见错误是只放名词或短语。例：...in case it rains.

slotSchema.label: 条件从句
slotSchema.hint: 用于if时，放真实条件。常见错误是把“预防目的”错写成普通条件。例：If it rains, we’ll stay inside.

commonErrors: Wrong: I’ll take an umbrella if it rains. 原因：这表示“下雨时我才会带伞”，不是“提前带着以防下雨”。改法：I’ll take an umbrella in case it rains.
Wrong: In case it will rain, take an umbrella. 原因：in case后的从句一般不用will表示普通将来可能。改法：In case it rains, take an umbrella.
Wrong: I brought snacks in case if you get hungry. 原因：in case和if不能直接叠在一起表示同一层关系。改法：I brought snacks in case you get hungry. 或 If you get hungry, have some snacks.
Wrong: I saved his number in case of he calls. 原因：in case of后面接名词短语，不接完整从句。改法：I saved his number in case he calls. 或 in case of an emergency.
Wrong: If an emergency, call me. 原因：if后面通常要有完整从句。改法：If there is an emergency, call me. 或 In case of an emergency, call me.
Wrong: We left early in case we would miss the train. 原因：这里是预防可能发生的事，in case后通常不用would。改法：We left early in case we missed the train./...in case we miss the train.

contrasts.title: in case vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接从句，但in case表示“先做准备”，if表示“满足条件才发生主句”。语气上，in case更像防患未然，if只是普通条件。对比：Take a map in case you get lost. / If you get lost, call me.

contrasts.title: if / unless
contrasts.note: 结构上unless=if...not，表示排除条件；in case不是否定条件。场景上，unless用于规则和限制，in case用于预防。对比：We’ll go if it’s sunny. / We won’t go unless it’s sunny. / Take a hat in case it’s sunny.

contrasts.title: if vs as long as / provided that
contrasts.note: 结构上as long as和provided that都表示“只要、前提是”，比if更强调条件；in case不强调前提，而强调准备。语气上provided that更正式。对比：You can go if you finish. / You can go provided that you finish. / Bring your notes in case you need them.

contrasts.title: in case / if vs even if / even though
contrasts.note: 结构上even if表示“即使”，even though表示“虽然”；它们都不是“预防”。场景上，even if谈让步，in case谈备用，if谈条件。对比：Even if it rains, we’ll go. / If it rains, we’ll stay home. / Take coats in case it rains.

examples.sentence: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
examples.translation: 带把伞，以防下雨。
examples.note: 最核心用法：先做准备，再说可能发生的事。

examples.sentence: In case it rains, take an umbrella.
examples.translation: 如果担心下雨，就带把伞以防万一。
examples.note: 句首也可以，用逗号隔开主句。

examples.sentence: Please keep this receipt in case you need to return the item.
examples.translation: 请保留这张小票，以防你需要退货。
examples.note: 常见于说明、提醒、服务场景。

examples.sentence: If it rains, we’ll cancel the picnic.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就取消野餐。
examples.note: 这里不是提前准备，而是真实条件触发结果。

examples.sentence: I wrote down the address in case my phone died.
examples.translation: 我记下了地址，以防手机没电。
examples.note: 日常生活中非常自然，强调备用措施。

examples.sentence: Call me if you have any questions.
examples.translation: 如果你有任何问题，就给我打电话。
examples.note: 普通条件，不要误换成in case。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 主句写准备动作，如 take, bring, keep, save, write down；从句写可能发生的情况，如 it rains, you need it
templates.exampleSentence: Bring some cash in case the card machine doesn't work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 带些现金，以防刷卡机不能用。

templates.label: 句首替代模板
slotHints: 适合提醒或建议；句首从句后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: In case you get cold, take a jacket.
templates.exampleTranslation: 以防你觉得冷，带上外套。

templates.label: 普通条件模板
slotHints: 前面写条件，后面写结果；不要误写成“提前准备”的意思
templates.exampleSentence: If you see Anna, tell her I called.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你见到安娜，告诉她我打过电话。

templates.label: 书面提醒模板
slotHints: 适合通知、服务、说明文字，语气礼貌清楚
templates.exampleSentence: Please save this email in case you need the booking number later.
templates.exampleTranslation: 请保存这封邮件，以防你之后需要预订号码。

items.title: when / while
items.coreExplanation: when和while都可引出时间从句。when常指“某个时间点/某件事发生时”，后面可接短动作或长状态；while更强调“在……期间、两个动作同时进行”，后面通常接持续性动作或状态。时间从句可放句首，也可放主句后；句首后通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都常见。when适合讲某事发生时、习惯性情况、故事推进；while适合描述两个动作重叠的背景。while放句首还常有“与此同时”感。初学者不要把while随便用于瞬间动作，也不要把它和表示转折的while/whereas混淆。时间从句在前时，后面一般加逗号；在后时通常不加。最安全做法：先写主句，再加when/while从句。

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 填when或while。when适合“某事发生时”；while适合“在……期间/同时”。常见错误：用while接瞬间动作作唯一主事件。例：When I arrived, she smiled./While I was cooking, he called.

slotSchema.label: 时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整主谓结构。when后可接短动作或状态；while后更自然的是进行中、持续中的动作或状态。常见错误：只写动词不写主语。例：when I got home / while she was studying

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 写主要事件或结果。常见错误：两个从句连在一起却没有完整主句。例：I saw Tom / the lights went out

commonErrors: Wrong: While I arrived, he left.｜错因：arrive是瞬间动作，while不自然地强调持续重叠。｜改法：When I arrived, he left.
Wrong: When I was cooking, the phone was ringing.｜错因：若想表达两个动作同时进行，when不如while自然。｜改法：While I was cooking, the phone rang./While I was cooking, he was washing the dishes.
Wrong: I was sleeping, when the doorbell rang.｜错因：主句在前时，这里通常不用逗号隔开when时间从句。｜改法：I was sleeping when the doorbell rang.
Wrong: While he was tired, he went to bed.｜错因：这里不是“在……期间”，而是“因为/由于”或“当……时”语义不清。｜改法：When he was tired, he went to bed. / Because he was tired, he went to bed.
Wrong: While I called her, she took a shower.｜错因：call常被理解为短动作，若表达“我打电话时她正在洗澡”，应用when引出时间点。｜改法：When I called her, she was taking a shower.
Wrong: When both children were doing homework, their mother cooked.｜错因：句子重点是两个持续动作重叠，while更贴切。｜改法：While the children were doing homework, their mother was cooking.

contrasts.title: when vs while：时间点 vs 同时进行
contrasts.note: 结构上，when后可接短动作或长状态；while后通常接持续性动作/状态。语感上，when像“……的时候”，while更像“在……期间，同时”。对比：When I opened the door, the cat ran out. / While I was opening the window, the cat was hiding.

contrasts.title: while（时间）vs whereas/while（对比）
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接从句，但时间while表示同时；对比while/whereas表示“而、却”。场景上，时间义常见于叙事；对比义更常见于说明比较。对比：While I was studying, he was cooking. / I like tea, while he prefers coffee.

contrasts.title: when vs once
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句；when是“当……时”，once是“一旦……就/……之后”。语气上，once更强调先后和条件感。对比：When you arrive, call me. / Once you arrive, call me.

contrasts.title: when vs as soon as
contrasts.note: 结构上都可接时间从句；as soon as强调“立刻、紧接着”，when只表示“在那个时候”。场景上，催促、安排常用as soon as。对比：When I got home, I took a shower. / As soon as I got home, I took a shower.

contrasts.title: 句首 vs 句中位置
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可句首或句中。语篇上，句首更像先交代时间背景，句中更像先说主事件。标点上，句首后常加逗号，句中通常不加。对比：While I was walking home, it started to rain. / It started to rain while I was walking home.

examples.sentence: When I got home, I took off my shoes.
examples.translation: 我到家时，脱了鞋。
examples.note: 最基础用法：when引出一个时间点。

examples.sentence: It started to rain while we were playing soccer.
examples.translation: 我们踢足球的时候，开始下雨了。
examples.note: 主句在前，while表示动作重叠。

examples.sentence: While the experiment was running, the team recorded the data.
examples.translation: 实验进行期间，团队记录了数据。
examples.note: 较偏书面，while用于背景过程。

examples.sentence: When I called her, she was taking a shower.
examples.translation: 我给她打电话时，她正在洗澡。
examples.note: 对比敏感：when接短动作called，主句写当时正在进行的动作。

examples.sentence: While my brother was cooking, I set the table.
examples.translation: 我哥哥做饭的时候，我摆好了餐桌。
examples.note: 两个动作同时发生，但不必都用进行时。

examples.sentence: When I was a child, we visited my grandparents every summer.
examples.translation: 我小时候，我们每年夏天都会去看望祖父母。
examples.note: when也可接较长时期，表示某阶段中的习惯。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: [timeClause]写一个发生的时间点，如I got home；[mainClause]写主要事件，如I called my mom
templates.exampleSentence: When I finished work, I went home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我下班后就回家了。

templates.label: 同时进行模板
slotHints: [timeClause]最好用持续动作或状态，如I was studying；[mainClause]写同时发生的另一动作
templates.exampleSentence: While I was studying, my roommate was watching TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我学习的时候，我室友在看电视。

templates.label: 主句在前模板
slotHints: 先写你最想表达的主事件；when后可接短动作，while后更适合持续动作
templates.exampleSentence: I met Lisa when I was living in Beijing.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我住在北京的时候认识了丽莎。

templates.label: 写作背景模板
slotHints: [backgroundClause]写背景过程；[eventClause]写在该过程中发生的事
templates.exampleSentence: While the meeting was going on, someone knocked on the door.
templates.exampleTranslation: 会议进行时，有人敲门了。

items.title: when / once / as soon as
items.pattern: when / once / as soon as + clause
items.coreExplanation: when、once、as soon as后面都接完整从句（主语+谓语），表示“到某时/一旦/一……就……”。它们常放句首引出条件或时间点，后面接主句；也可放主句后。when最宽，once强调某事发生后，as soon as强调紧接着马上发生。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性，常见于说明步骤、安排计划、日常承诺、规则提醒。when适合一般时间触发，once适合“完成后再做下一步”，as soon as适合“立刻”。不适合单独接名词，也不要和进行中的while乱换。放句首时时间从句后通常加逗号；放句中一般不加。初学者最安全的做法是：先写时间从句，再写主句。

slotSchema.label: 时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整从句：主语+动词，如 you get home / the movie ends。常见错误是只放名词，如 × when your arrival。可说：when you arrive。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放结果或下一步，如 call me / we can start。常见错误是主句不完整，如 × as soon as he arrives, immediately。可说：as soon as he arrives, we’ll leave。

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 只能放 when / once / as soon as 之一。常见错误是按中文直译乱加 soon，如 × when he arrives soon。要么用 when he arrives，要么用 as soon as he arrives。

commonErrors: × When arriving home, call me. → 这里把本卡基础结构“连接词+完整从句”写成了分词结构，初学阶段不稳。✓ When you get home, call me.
× As soon as you will arrive, text me. → 时间从句里一般不用 will 表将来，英语常用一般现在时表示将来。✓ As soon as you arrive, text me.
× Once finish your homework, you can play. → once 后要接完整从句，不是裸动词。✓ Once you finish your homework, you can play.
× I will call you when will I get there. → when 后如果表示时间，不用疑问语序。✓ I will call you when I get there.
× As soon as the meeting, we’ll leave. → as soon as 后不能直接接名词短语，要接从句。✓ As soon as the meeting ends, we’ll leave.
× When you get home. Call me. → 时间从句不能单独成句，必须连主句；句号把结构打断了。✓ When you get home, call me.

contrasts.title: when vs once
contrasts.note: 结构上都接完整从句；when只是“当……时/每当……时”，once更像“一旦……就；在……之后就”。语气上，once更强调先完成前件，再进入后件，常见于步骤说明。对比：When you get home, call me.（到家时打）/ Once you get home, call me.（一到家就打）

contrasts.title: once vs as soon as
contrasts.note: 结构都可放句首或句中。once强调“某事成立后”，as soon as强调“紧接着、不要拖”。场景上，once适合流程、规则；as soon as适合催促、即时反应。对比：Once you finish, send it. / As soon as you finish, send it.

contrasts.title: when vs while
contrasts.note: when后可接短动作或长动作；while更常接持续中的动作，常见进行状态。语气上，while强调“在……过程中”，不是“触发下一步”。对比：When the bell rings, leave. / While the teacher is speaking, don’t talk.

contrasts.title: vs before / after / by the time
contrasts.note: 这些词也谈时间，但结构和焦点不同：before/after强调先后关系，by the time强调“到……为止的结果”。when/once/as soon as更像“时间触发器”。对比：After you arrive, call me.（到了之后）/ As soon as you arrive, call me.（一到就打）

contrasts.title: time clause tense
contrasts.note: 和汉语不同，表示将来时，这类时间从句里通常不用 will，而主句可用 will。这个限制同样适用于 when、once、as soon as。对比：✓ When she comes, we’ll start. / × When she will come, we’ll start.

examples.sentence: When you get home, call me.
examples.translation: 你到家时给我打电话。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：句首时间从句 + 逗号 + 主句。

examples.sentence: I’ll text you once I arrive.
examples.translation: 我一到就给你发短信。
examples.note: 时间从句放在主句后面时通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: As soon as you finish the form, send it to the office.
examples.translation: 你一填完表，就把它发到办公室。
examples.note: 强调“紧接着马上做下一步”，很适合指示或流程。

examples.sentence: When the movie ends, we can talk, but as soon as my phone rings, I need to answer it.
examples.translation: 电影结束后我们可以聊，但我的手机一响，我就得接。
examples.note: 同句对比：when只是到某时；as soon as强调立刻反应。

examples.sentence: Once the data is ready, the report will be published.
examples.translation: 数据一准备好，报告就会发布。
examples.note: 较书面，常见于工作或说明文字。

examples.sentence: As soon as the teacher comes in, everyone gets quiet.
examples.translation: 老师一进来，大家就安静下来。
examples.note: 日常规律性场景，也可以理解为“每次一……就……”。

templates.label: 安全入门模板
templates.template: When/Once/As soon as + 从句, + 主句
slotHints: 从句写主语+一般现在时动词，如 you get home
主句写结果或下一步，如 call me / we’ll start
templates.exampleSentence: When you finish dinner, wash the dishes.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你吃完晚饭后，把碗洗了。

templates.label: 后置模板
templates.template: 主句 + when/once/as soon as + 从句
slotHints: 主句先写主要信息，如 I’ll text you
后面的时间从句仍用完整从句，不用 will
templates.exampleSentence: I’ll call you once I get there.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我一到那儿就给你打电话。

templates.label: 书面流程模板
templates.template: Once + 事项完成从句, + 主句
slotHints: 从句常用 finish, complete, be ready, be approved 等
主句写正式的下一步，如 will be sent / can begin
templates.exampleSentence: Once the payment is confirmed, your order will be processed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 付款一确认，您的订单就会处理。

templates.label: 即时反应模板
templates.template: As soon as + 触发从句, + 立即动作主句
slotHints: 触发从句写刚发生的事，如 he arrives / the bell rings
主句常配 leave, reply, call, start 等即时动作
templates.exampleSentence: As soon as the bell rings, the students leave the classroom.
templates.exampleTranslation: 铃一响，学生们就离开教室。

items.title: before / after / by the time
items.coreExplanation: before、after、by the time都用来连接两个时间相关的动作。它们后面通常接完整从句（主语+动词），表示“在……之前”“在……之后”“到……的时候”。这类时间从句可放句首，也可放主句后；放句首时后面常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气通常是neutral，口语和书面语都很常见。适合讲先后顺序、安排计划、描述到某时已发生的情况。by the time尤其常用于“截止某时”的结果判断。不要把它们直接当介词后面只接一个完整句子碎片，也不要和until混用。句首时注意逗号；初学者最安全的做法是先写“Before/After/By the time + 从句, 主句”。

slotSchema.label: 时间连接词
slotSchema.hint: 填before / after / by the time。常见错误是按中文直译乱换；例如截止某时用by the time更自然：By the time I arrived, they had left.

slotSchema.label: 时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整从句，通常是主语+动词，如 I get home / the meeting started。常见错误是只放短语：误 Before dinner, I finish it.（可说但结构不同）；本卡先学 Before I eat dinner, I’ll finish it.

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放主要结果或动作，如 I will call you / they had left。常见错误是时态搭配随便写；例如 By the time we got there, the movie had started.

commonErrors: Wrong: Before to leave, check the door.｜错因：before后本卡重点接完整从句，不接to不定式。｜改法：Before you leave, check the door.
Wrong: After I arrived, then I called her.｜错因：after已经表示先后，再加then常显得重复累赘。｜改法：After I arrived, I called her.
Wrong: By the time I arrived, they leave.｜错因：到达时他们已经离开，主句常要用完成时表示“更早已发生”。｜改法：By the time I arrived, they had left.
Wrong: Until I got home, I took a shower.｜错因：until表示“直到……才/一直到……为止”，不表示简单先后。｜改法：After I got home, I took a shower.
Wrong: Before, I finish my homework.｜错因：before后缺少明确从句或时间对象，句子不完整。｜改法：Before I watch TV, I finish my homework.
Wrong: By the time tomorrow, I will finish it.｜错因：by the time后一般接从句，不直接接名词时间点。｜改法：By tomorrow, I will finish it. / By the time tomorrow comes, I will have finished it.

contrasts.title: before vs after
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接从句，但before表示主句动作早于从句，after表示晚于从句。语气都中性，常用于叙事和计划。对比：Before I sleep, I read. / After I sleep, I dream.

contrasts.title: by the time vs until
contrasts.note: by the time后接从句，强调“到某时已经/将会”；until强调动作持续到某时。场景上，by the time更像截止点，until更像持续线。对比：By the time he arrived, we had eaten. / We waited until he arrived.

contrasts.title: after vs when
contrasts.note: after明确表示先后顺序；when只说“当……时”，不一定强调谁先谁后。口气上when更宽泛，after更明确。对比：When I got home, I cooked. / After I got home, I cooked.

contrasts.title: after vs as soon as
contrasts.note: 两者都可接从句；after只表示“之后”，时间间隔可长可短，as soon as强调“立刻、一……就……”。后者更强烈、更口语化。对比：After he arrived, we started. / As soon as he arrived, we started.

contrasts.title: by the time vs before
contrasts.note: before看的是“在某动作之前”，by the time看的是“到某时间点时的状态/结果”。叙事中by the time常配完成时，更突出已完成。对比：Before he came, I left. / By the time he came, I had left.

examples.sentence: Before I go to bed, I brush my teeth.
examples.translation: 睡觉前，我会刷牙。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：Before + 从句, 主句。

examples.sentence: I always check the map before I drive to a new place.
examples.translation: 去新的地方开车前，我总会先看地图。
examples.note: 时间从句放句末也很自然，前面通常不用逗号。

examples.sentence: After the meeting ended, we sent the report.
examples.translation: 会议结束后，我们把报告发出去了。
examples.note: 适合写邮件、工作记录等较正式场景。

examples.sentence: By the time we got to the station, the train had left.
examples.translation: 等我们到车站时，火车已经开走了。
examples.note: 典型搭配：by the time + 一般过去时，主句常用过去完成时。

examples.sentence: After I finish dinner, I’ll call you.
examples.translation: 我吃完晚饭后给你打电话。
examples.note: 和as soon as相比，这里只表示之后，不强调立刻。

examples.sentence: By the time you read this email, I will be on the plane.
examples.translation: 等你读到这封邮件时，我已经在飞机上了。
examples.note: 常见于通知、留言，表示到某时的状态。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
templates.template: Before/After + 时间从句, + 主句
slotHints: 时间从句写“主语+动词”，如 I leave / class ends；主句写主要动作，如 I will call you。
templates.exampleSentence: After I get home, I will take a shower.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我到家后会洗澡。

templates.label: 句末时间从句模板
templates.template: 主句 + before/after + 时间从句
slotHints: 先写主句，再补充发生时间；适合口语和日常表达。
templates.exampleSentence: I do my homework before I watch TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我看电视前先做作业。

templates.label: 截止时间模板
templates.template: By the time + 时间从句, + 主句
slotHints: 用来写“到……的时候已经/将会……”；如果主句动作更早完成，常用完成时。
templates.exampleSentence: By the time she arrived, we had started dinner.
templates.exampleTranslation: 等她到的时候，我们已经开始吃晚饭了。

templates.label: 写作通知模板
templates.template: By the time + 时间从句, + 状态主句
slotHints: 常用于邮件、通知、留言，主句可写be动词状态，如 I will be at the office。
templates.exampleSentence: By the time you see this note, I will be in class.
templates.exampleTranslation: 等你看到这张便条时，我已经在上课了。

items.title: until / by the time
items.pattern: until + clause / by the time + clause
items.coreExplanation: until表示“持续到某个时间点才结束/变化”，前面通常接持续性的动作或状态；by the time表示“到……的时候，某情况已经发生或将已完成”。两者都常放句首引导时间从句，也可放主句后；从句后接完整句子，不接单个名词。
items.usageNote: 语气一般是neutral，口语和写作都常见。until适合描述等待、停留、工作到几点等“持续到终点”的情况；by the time适合截止时间、进度结果、到某时已经怎样。不要把by the time当成单纯“当……时”来用，也不要在until后随便接瞬间动作表达“到时就做”。句首从句后常加逗号；句中一般不加。初学者最安全：I’ll wait until… / By the time…, I will have…

slotSchema.label: 时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整主谓结构，如 you arrive, the meeting ends。常见错误是只放名词如 until tomorrow；本卡重点是从句型。例：until you arrive / by the time the class starts

slotSchema.label: 主句动作或结果
slotSchema.hint: until前面的主句通常是持续性动作/状态，如 wait, stay, work；by the time后面的主句常是已经完成或已经存在的结果。常见错误：until后主句用瞬间动作。例：I waited... / I had finished...

slotSchema.label: 时态选择
slotSchema.hint: until常直接配一般现在/过去/将来语境；by the time常和完成时连用表示“到那时已经”。常见错误：By the time he arrived, I finish. 应改为 had finished。例：By the time he arrived, I had finished.

commonErrors: Wrong: I will wait by the time you arrive. —— 错在把by the time当“等到”为止；它强调“到那时已经……”。Fix: I will wait until you arrive. / By the time you arrive, I will have left.
Wrong: Until you arrive, I will leave. —— 错在leave是瞬间动作，不适合表示“持续到……”。Fix: Until you arrive, I will stay here. / I won’t leave until you arrive.
Wrong: By the time he came, I finished my homework. —— 错在缺少“到那时已经完成”的时态。Fix: By the time he came, I had finished my homework.
Wrong: until tomorrow I finish it. —— 错在语序和结构都不自然；until引导时间点时应放在主句后，或用从句型。Fix: I’ll finish it by tomorrow. / I’ll work on it until tomorrow.
Wrong: By the time when she arrived, we ate. —— 错在by the time后不接when，且主句时态不对。Fix: By the time she arrived, we had eaten.
Wrong: I studied by the time midnight. —— 错在by the time后不能直接接名词时间点。Fix: I studied until midnight. / By the time it was midnight, I had finished studying.

contrasts.title: until vs by the time
contrasts.note: 结构上，until前后表示“持续到终点”；by the time后面给出参照时点，主句说“到那时已经怎样”。语气上，until重过程终止，by the time重截止结果。对比：I stayed until noon. / By the time it was noon, I had left.

contrasts.title: by the time vs when
contrasts.note: 结构上，when只是“当……时”；by the time是“到……的时候已经”。场景上，when适合一般同时发生，by the time适合进度领先或延后。对比：When he arrived, I was cooking. / By the time he arrived, I had cooked.

contrasts.title: until vs before
contrasts.note: 结构上，until表示一直持续到某点；before表示在某点之前。语义上，until强调终点，before只强调先后。对比：Wait until 6.（等到6点）/ Finish it before 6.（6点前完成）

contrasts.title: by the time vs before
contrasts.note: 结构上，before后可直接说“在……之前做”；by the time后常说“到……时已经做完”。语气上，before更像要求时限，by the time更像描述状态。对比：Finish it before he arrives. / By the time he arrives, I will have finished it.

contrasts.title: until vs not...until
contrasts.note: 结构上，until可直接接持续动作；not...until常接瞬间动作，表示“直到……才”。场景上，not...until更强调延迟发生。对比：I stayed until 10. / I didn’t leave until 10.

examples.sentence: I will wait until you arrive.
examples.translation: 我会一直等到你到。
examples.note: 最安全的until基础句：主句用持续动作wait。

examples.sentence: Until the rain stops, we’ll stay inside.
examples.translation: 雨停之前，我们会一直待在里面。
examples.note: 时间从句放句首时，后面加逗号。

examples.sentence: By the time the meeting starts, I will have printed the reports.
examples.translation: 到会议开始的时候，我就已经把报告打印好了。
examples.note: 写作和工作场景中很常见，突出截止前已完成。

examples.sentence: When he arrived, I was tired; by the time he arrived, I had finished everything.
examples.translation: 当他到的时候，我很累；到他到的时候，我已经把一切都做完了。
examples.note: 对比when和by the time：前者只是当时，后者强调“到那时已经”。

examples.sentence: I didn’t go to bed until midnight.
examples.translation: 我直到半夜才睡觉。
examples.note: not...until适合瞬间动作go to bed。

examples.sentence: By the time you get home, dinner will be ready.
examples.translation: 等你到家时，晚饭就会准备好了。
examples.note: 日常生活里表示“到那时结果已就绪”。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: [mainAction] 放持续动作，如 wait/stay/work；不要用 leave/arrive 这类瞬间动作
[timeClause] 放完整句子，如 you arrive / the class ends
templates.exampleSentence: I will stay here until you call me.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我会一直待在这里，直到你给我打电话。

templates.label: 句首时间从句模板
slotHints: 句首从句后加逗号
主句仍尽量用持续动作或状态，如 we will stay / I will be here
templates.exampleSentence: Until the bus comes, we will wait here.
templates.exampleTranslation: 公交车来之前，我们会一直在这里等。

templates.label: 截止前已完成模板
slotHints: 适合未来某时点前完成的事
[timeClause] 用一般现在表达将来，如 he arrives，不说 he will arrive
templates.exampleSentence: By the time he arrives, I will have finished the email.
templates.exampleTranslation: 等他到的时候，我就已经把邮件写完了。

templates.label: 过去截止点模板
slotHints: 用来回顾过去：到过去某时已经完成
主句常用had finished/had left/had eaten等
templates.exampleSentence: By the time she got there, we had left.
templates.exampleTranslation: 等她到那儿的时候，我们已经走了。

items.title: that / whether / if (noun clause)
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构把一个完整句子变成名词性内容，放在动词、形容词或名词后面作“所说/所想/是否”的内容。that后接陈述句，whether/if后接一般疑问意义的从句。最常见位置是主句谓语后，如 think, know, ask, wonder 后。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性；口语和书面语都常见。适合表达看法、转述信息、说明自己知道/不知道、问是否发生。that 常可省略，尤其口语中放在 say/think/know 后；whether 比 if 更正式，也更适合写作、句首主语、不定式前和介词后。初学者先记：陈述内容用 that，是否用 whether/if。若从句很长，注意不要漏主语和谓语。

slotSchema.label: 主句触发词
slotSchema.hint: 可放 think, say, know, ask, wonder, be sure 等。常见错误：ask 后误接 that 表示“是否”；若是问是否，应接 whether/if。例：I wonder whether... / She said that...

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 只能按意思选 that / whether / if。that 表示“……这件事”，whether/if 表示“是否”。常见错误：把 yes-no 内容写成 that。例：I know that he is busy. / I don't know if he is busy.

slotSchema.label: 从句内容
slotSchema.hint: 后面要是完整句子，有主语和谓语。常见错误：只放短语，如 that happy。应写 that he is happy。例：whether she can come / that they left early

commonErrors: I don't know that he will come.｜若你想表达“我不知道他是否会来”，that 不能表示“是否”。｜改为：I don't know whether/if he will come.
She asked that he was ready.｜ask 表示询问 yes-no 时通常不用 that-clause。｜改为：She asked whether/if he was ready.
Whether he will come is unclear to me.｜句子本身对，但初学者若放在句首容易写乱；而且 if 不能这样自然地作句首主语。｜更稳妥：It is unclear whether he will come.
It depends on if we have time.｜介词 on 后正式英语更自然用 whether，不用 if。｜改为：It depends on whether we have time.
I wonder if or not he agrees.｜or not 的位置错了。｜改为：I wonder whether or not he agrees. / I wonder if he agrees or not.
I think whether he is right.｜think 后通常接陈述内容，不直接接 whether 表示“认为是否”。｜改为：I think that he is right. / I am not sure whether he is right.

contrasts.title: that vs whether/if
contrasts.note: 结构上，that 后是陈述内容；whether/if 后是“是否”内容。语气上，that 只是转述事实，whether/if 带不确定或选择判断。对比：I know that he is here.（我知道他在）/ I don't know whether he is here.（我不知道他在不在）

contrasts.title: whether vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上二者都可引导“是否”从句，但 whether 能用于更广：句首主语、介词后、不定式前；if 常用于口语、动词后。语体上 whether 更正式。对比：Whether he comes matters. / I don't know if he will come.

contrasts.title: whether or not
contrasts.note: 结构上 whether 可直接搭配 or not，强调“两种情况都考虑”；if 通常不直接接 if or not。语气上 whether or not 更完整、更书面。对比：Let me know whether or not you can come. / Let me know if you can come.

contrasts.title: that-clause vs what-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上 that-clause 不自带缺失成分，只是完整陈述；what-clause 本身含“……的东西/事情”，从句里少宾语或补语。语义上不同。对比：I know that he said it. / I know what he said.

contrasts.title: that can be omitted
contrasts.note: 结构上 that 在很多动词后可省略，如 think, say, know；但 whether/if 不能这样省。语气上省略 that 更口语。对比：I think (that) she is right. / I wonder whether she is right. 不能省 whether

examples.sentence: I think that this plan will work.
examples.translation: 我认为这个计划会成功。
examples.note: 最基础：that 引导陈述内容；口语里 that 可省略。

examples.sentence: Do you know if he is at home?
examples.translation: 你知道他是否在家吗？
examples.note: 动词后常用 if 表示“是否”，很口语自然。

examples.sentence: Whether the policy will succeed remains unclear.
examples.translation: 这项政策是否会成功仍不明确。
examples.note: 句首主语位置用 whether，更适合书面表达。

examples.sentence: It depends on whether we can get enough time.
examples.translation: 这取决于我们是否能有足够的时间。
examples.note: 介词 on 后用 whether，不用 if。

examples.sentence: She said that she had finished the report.
examples.translation: 她说她已经完成报告了。
examples.note: 转述别人说的话，用 that-clause。

examples.sentence: I'm not sure whether he is right.
examples.translation: 我不确定他是否是对的。
examples.note: 和 I think that he is right 对比明显：一个是不确定，一个是判断。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
templates.template: I don't know whether/if + 从句.
slotHints: 前面固定用 I don't know；后面放完整句子，如 he is busy / she can come。若想更正式，优先用 whether。
templates.exampleSentence: I don't know whether she can come.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不知道她是否能来。

templates.label: 常用陈述内容模板
slotHints: 主句常用 I/we/they；that 可省略，但初学者保留更稳。后面必须是完整陈述句。
templates.exampleSentence: We know that the train is late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们知道火车晚点了。

templates.label: 替代位置模板
slotHints: 用于句首主语时用 whether，不用 if。主句常接 matters, is unclear, depends on...
templates.exampleSentence: Whether he agrees matters a lot.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他是否同意非常重要。

templates.label: 写作模板
templates.template: It + be + adjective/noun + whether + 从句.
slotHints: 适合写作和正式表达，如 unclear, uncertain, the question。比直接用 if 更稳妥。
templates.exampleSentence: It is unclear whether the plan will succeed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个计划是否会成功还不清楚。

items.title: what-clause / that-clause
items.coreExplanation: what-clause 表示“……的事/内容”，what 本身就在从句里充当主语、宾语等，所以后面必须是完整但缺少一个成分的从句。that-clause 只是陈述“……这件事”，that 不充当成分，后面要接结构完整的句子。两者都常作主语、宾语、表语，最常见在动词后。
items.usageNote: 语体上两者都很常用；that-clause 更中性、适合陈述观点与转述事实，what-clause 更强调“具体内容/实际发生的事”，口语和写作都自然。适合说明、汇报、解释、讨论想法；不适合拿 what 直接代替关系代词修饰名词。句首作主语时后面常接谓语，句中作宾语最稳。初学者先记：know/see/explain + what...；think/say/believe + that...。
sceneTags: 说明自己知道/看到的内容

slotSchema.label: 主句动词
slotSchema.hint: 放能接名词性从句的动词，如 know, say, think, explain, see。常错：把所有动词都接 what/that。例：I know ... / She explained ...

slotSchema.label: what 从句
slotSchema.hint: 放“what + 缺一个成分的从句”。what 在从句里可作主语或宾语。常错：后面再加一个重复宾语。例：what he said；不是 what he said it

slotSchema.label: that 从句
slotSchema.hint: 放“that + 完整句子”。that 不作成分。常错：在 that 从句里缺主语或宾语。例：that he was tired；不是 that was tired

slotSchema.label: 句中位置
slotSchema.hint: 最安全放在动词后作宾语；也可句首作主语。常错：长 what/that 从句乱插在名词前。例：What he said surprised me.

commonErrors: I know that he said.｜错因：that 从句必须是完整句，he said 后缺宾语，不能用 that。｜改法：I know what he said.
She explained what the problem was true.｜错因：把 what-clause 和补语硬连在一起，结构混乱。｜改法：She explained that the problem was real. / She explained what the problem was.
What he said it surprised me.｜错因：what 已经作 said 的宾语，it 重复。｜改法：What he said surprised me.
I think what he is honest.｜错因：what 不能引出完整判断句“他很诚实”；这里不缺成分。｜改法：I think that he is honest.
The book what I bought is new.｜错因：这里是在修饰名词 book，是定语从句，不是名词性从句。｜改法：The book that/which I bought is new.
She told me that what happened.｜错因：that 后不能直接接疑似残缺结构；若表达“发生了什么”，要直接用 what-clause。｜改法：She told me what happened. / She told me that something happened.

contrasts.title: what-clause vs that-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，what 从句内部缺一个成分；that 从句内部完整。语气上，what 更像“具体内容是什么”，that 更像“某个事实/判断”。对比：I know what he wants.（我知道他想要什么）/ I know that he wants help.（我知道他想要帮助）

contrasts.title: what-clause vs whether/if-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，what 问“什么内容”，whether/if 问“是否”。场景上，what 关注信息内容，whether/if 关注二选一或不确定。对比：I asked what he wanted. / I asked whether he wanted it.

contrasts.title: what-clause vs relative clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，what-clause 本身就是一个名词性整体；关系从句要先有被修饰名词。场景上，what 用来当“……的东西/事情”，关系从句用来描述某个名词。对比：What I need is time. / The thing that I need is time.

contrasts.title: that-clause vs zero that
contrasts.note: 结构上，that 在很多宾语从句里可省略，但句首作主语时通常不能随便省。语体上，省略 that 更口语，保留 that 更清楚。对比：I think (that) he is right. / That he is right is obvious.

contrasts.title: what-clause 作主语 vs 作宾语
contrasts.note: 结构上都可以，但宾语位置更常见、更易写。语篇上，句首主语更像书面总结，动词后宾语更自然。对比：What she wrote was helpful. / I read what she wrote.

examples.sentence: I know what he said.
examples.translation: 我知道他说了什么。
examples.note: 最核心对比：said 后缺宾语，所以用 what。

examples.sentence: I think that he is right.
examples.translation: 我认为他是对的。
examples.note: that 从句表达完整判断，that 不作成分。

examples.sentence: What she needs is more time.
examples.translation: 她需要的是更多时间。
examples.note: what-clause 放句首作主语，较像总结说明。

examples.sentence: That the plan failed was obvious.
examples.translation: 这个计划失败这件事是明显的。
examples.note: 书面感较强；正式写作中可见。

examples.sentence: I understand what you mean, but I don't think that we can do it today.
examples.translation: 我明白你的意思，但我认为我们今天做不了。
examples.note: 同句对比：what 表“你表达的内容”，that 表“一个判断”。

examples.sentence: She explained what happened after class.
examples.translation: 她课后解释了发生了什么。
examples.note: 日常很自然；explain 后常接 what-clause。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: what 后接缺成分的从句，如 what he said / what you need；不要再补重复宾语
templates.exampleSentence: I know what you need.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我知道你需要什么。

templates.label: 常用判断模板
slotHints: that 后放完整句，如 he is right / we can win；不确定时先不要省 that
templates.exampleSentence: I think that she is busy.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我觉得她很忙。

templates.label: 句首主语模板
templates.template: What + 从句 + be + 名词/形容词
slotHints: 用来总结“……的事”；what 从句内部仍要缺一个成分，如 What I need is ...
templates.exampleSentence: What I need is a break.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我需要的是休息一下。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: That + 完整句 + be + 形容词
slotHints: 较正式，适合书面说明事实或结论；that 从句作主语时不要省略 that
templates.exampleSentence: That he refused was surprising.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他拒绝了，这很令人惊讶。

items.title: whether or not / whether / if
items.shortLabel: whether vs if vs whether or not
items.pattern: whether or not / whether / if + clause
items.coreExplanation: whether 和 if 都可引出“是否……”的名词从句；whether or not 强调“不管是否都成立”。后面通常接完整主谓句。它们常放在动词、形容词、介词后作宾语，也可放句首作主语从句；正式写作里 whether 更稳妥。
items.usageNote: 语气上，if 较口语、whether 较中性到正式、whether or not 最强调“无论是否”。适合表达不确定、做决定、说明某事与条件无关；不适合拿 whether or not 去表示“即使”让步。放在介词后、to do 前、句首主语从句时，初学者优先用 whether，不要用 if。若 whether or not 放句中，or not 通常紧跟 whether，也可放到从句末，但别两处都放。
sceneTags: 邮件或报告中的正式表达
说明结果不受条件影响

slotSchema.label: 前置成分
slotSchema.hint: 可放动词、形容词、名词或介词短语，如 know, decide, depend on。常见错误是介词后用 if；应改为 whether。例：It depends on whether he agrees.

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 可用 whether / if / whether or not。常见错误是把 whether or not 当 even if；它表示“是否都……”，不是“即使……也……”。例：We'll go whether or not it rains.

slotSchema.label: 从句
slotSchema.hint: 后面放完整陈述语序的句子，不用疑问语序。常见错误：whether is he ready；应为 whether he is ready。例：I don't know whether he is ready.

commonErrors: I’m not sure if or not he will come.｜错因：if 一般不直接和 or not 连用成 if or not。｜改法：用 whether or not he will come，或 if he will come。
It depends on if we have time.｜错因：介词 on 后接名词从句时，标准用法用 whether，不用 if。｜改法：It depends on whether we have time.
I wonder whether or not will she join us.｜错因：名词从句里不能倒装，必须用陈述语序。｜改法：I wonder whether or not she will join us.
Whether or not it rains, but we will go.｜错因：whether or not 引导让步/无关条件时，主句前通常不用 but。｜改法：Whether or not it rains, we will go.
I don’t know whether to he go.｜错因：whether 后接不定式时应是 whether to do，不是 whether to + 主语 + 动词。｜改法：I don’t know whether to go.
If to accept the offer is difficult.｜错因：to do 前不能用 if；这种结构要用 whether。｜改法：Whether to accept the offer is difficult.

contrasts.title: whether vs if：普通“是否”
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可接完整从句；但 if 更口语，whether 更正式。场景上，日常对话可常用 if，写作和考试里 whether 更安全。对比：I don’t know if he’s free. / I don’t know whether he’s free.

contrasts.title: 介词后：用 whether，不用 if
contrasts.note: 结构上，介词后接名词从句时通常只能用 whether。语气上更书面、更标准，常见于 depend on, worry about。对比：It depends on whether we win. ×depends on if we win.

contrasts.title: whether or not vs whether：强调强度不同
contrasts.note: 结构上，whether or not 比单独 whether 多了 or not。语义上，whether 只是提出“是否”，whether or not 强调“无论是否都如此”。对比：Ask whether he agrees. / We will leave whether or not he agrees.

contrasts.title: whether or not 不等于 even if
contrasts.note: 结构上，whether or not 表示两种情况都包括；even if 表示“即使在某种不利情况也……”。语气上，even if 更像让步，whether or not 更像与条件无关。对比：We’ll go whether or not it rains. / We’ll go even if it rains.

contrasts.title: whether vs that：是否 vs 事实
